sourceMovie.title: Yoroï
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Yoroï (2025) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Tomaszewski's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mortal Kombat
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Yoroï clicked for you, Mortal Kombat (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Yoroï?
faq.answer: Try Mortal Kombat, Monster Hunter, The Forbidden Kingdom—each shares DNA with Yoroï while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Yoroï?
faq.answer: Yoroï (2025) is best described as Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Yoroï worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
